events.title: EP Stockshots

items.title: EU - China relations
items.summary: China is the EU's second-largest trading partner for trade in goods alone, after the United States. For the EU, ensuring reciprocity, achieving a level-playing field, and addressing asymmetries in the relationship are matters of priority. The EU remains committed to addressing these challenges through dialogue with China and underscores the importance of the WTO as the best avenue to address the root causes of the current imbalance. This infoclip shows footage of high-level meetings between political leaders from the European Union and China, including representatives of the European Parliament and the Presidents of the European Commission and the European Council. It also contains general shots of Beijing and Shanghai, along with Chinese companies such as Temu and Alibaba. In addition, it includes images of product packaging and views of the port of Antwerp.

items.title: General views of Beijing (China)
items.summary: This Infoclip presents general views of Beijing (China).

items.title: Online protection of minors and young people
items.summary: This infoclip shows young children watching videos and playing games on mobile devices and computers, parents searching on the internet for parental control tools and creating children’s friendly profiles on viewing platforms and gaming platforms, parents checking the content their children are accessing and setting up restrictions such as screen time restrictions on devices. It is also illustrating parents attending a training on online protection of children (provided by Child focus organisation). A growing number of children are navigating digital environments not designed for them. There is therefore a discussion on whether to enhance the protection of minors online. The Internal Market and Consumer Protection Committee (IMCO) has adopted an own-initiative report with recommendations for safer digital environments for minors.

items.title: Women’s Rights - Combating Disinformation and Violence in the Digital Age
items.summary: Online threats, cyber violence and disinformation, including deepfakes and AI‑generated content, contribute to broader pressures on democracy by undermining trust and can silence voices, including women’s by restraining their freedom of expression. Violence targeting women in politics leads to under‑representation. The EU and the European Parliament in particular, has recognised the seriousness of such phenomena and reinforces its efforts to support women in their efforts to overcome these serious obstacles to political participation. This infoclip illustrates women and young girls browsing through social media platforms, journalists verifying information using online tools, female journalists preparing and recording or broadcasting shows for various media and female leaders and politicians acting in their official capacities in different occasions/ events.

items.title: Digital Services Act (DSA), E-Commerce and Social Media
items.summary: This infoclip gives an overview of tech giants such as Amazon, Meta, Google, TikTok, LinkedIn and X. In 2025 Meta announced the end of its third party fact-checking program. In 2024 the Commission opened proceedings against TikTok for not having assessed risks linked to election integrity during the recent Romanian presidential elections. The Commission also proceeded against X and preliminary findings confirmed its breach of the DSA. This infoclip shows a woman buying on Amazon, people scrolling on Social media, and offices building of TikTok (Source : Tiktok).

items.title: Protecting EU citizens from tobacco smoke and aerosol exposure
items.summary: On 27 November 2024, during the plenary session in Brussels; MEPs discuss the Commission's proposal for a Council recommendation on smoke and aerosol-free environments, to replace Council Recommendation 2009/C296/02.The proposed recommendation calls on Member States to extend the coverage of smoke free environment policies to key outdoor areas, including: outdoor recreational areas for children (public playgrounds, amusement parks, swimming pools, zoos and other similar outdoor spaces); outdoor areas connected to healthcare and education premises (hospitals, health centres, schools, training centres); outdoor areas associated to a place of work; public buildings; service establishments (restaurants, bars, cafés); transport stops and stations (bus, tram, train, airports). It also recommends that Member States extend smoke-free policies to emerging products such as electronic cigarettes.

items.title: General views of Tbilisi (Georgia)
items.summary: This infoclip shows images of Tbilisi (capital city of Georgia). In particular we provide footage of the city centre of the Georgian capital, including views of prominent public buildings such as the Georgian Parliament, the Presidential Palace and the House of Justice.

items.title: Energy challenges in the EU
items.summary: Ongoing instability in Iran and the disruption of maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz have brought about a sharp increase in the cost of electricity and gas, as well as fuels such as petrol and diesel. This has had a direct effect on household energy bills and on European companies’ operating costs. This infoclip features illustrations of customers paying at petrol stations, highlights the rising cost of living, and shows the use of heating, gas, and electrical appliances, as well as the transport of goods and trade.

items.title: Air passengers rights (extended version)
items.summary: This infoclip presents different stages of air travel. It illustrates situations commonly experienced by travellers, including departures and arrivals, hand luggage, check-in procedures, security controls and aircraft operations. The footage intend to offer a broad, illustrative background to the topic of air passenger rights. The Video may not be used in any way to denigrate or damage the image of Aeroporti di Roma and the Mundys Group Companies.

items.title: Maritime trade
items.summary: The infoclip features the Antwerp’s commercial harbour, showing container barges transporting cars and other goods into containers. It also highlights tugboat maneuvers ships through the busy port. Aerial drone shots provide general overviews of the harbour’s scale and activity. From street-level views captured from a car, we see the trucks transporting goods into the port. The footage also highlights modern wind turbines. Shipping remains one of the most important means of trade for the Member States of the EU. Nowadays, ships carry more than half the value of goods imports to the EU and over 40 percent of goods exports from the EU. In 2021, about 1.1 trillion euros worth of goods were imported to the EU, and about 956 billion euros worth of cargo exported from the EU by sea.

items.title: EP Stockshots
items.summary: Infoclip: electrification plan

items.title: New genomic techniques
items.summary: NGTs are a variety of techniques that alter the genetic material of an organism. Currently in the EU, all plants obtained by NGTs are subject to the same rules as GMOs, which are among the strictest in the world. NGTs could help to make our food system more sustainable and resilient by developing improved plant varieties that are climate resilient, pest resistant, and give higher yields or that require fewer fertilisers and pesticides. This news item shows extracts from a CSIC laboratory, plant research, scientists at work, in vitro samples and genetically modified plant cultures.

events.title: EP Stockshots

items.title: Circularity of the design and end-of-life of vehicles
items.summary: This Infoclip shows views of trafic and cars, a car factory, tests of a car prototype, a vehicle ricycling center, a collecting center and supplying of recycled car parts. The European Parliament discusses new rules covering the entire vehicle lifecycle, from design to end-of-life. This Infoclip show various steps of the car lifecycle, from manufactoring to recycling. In 2023, the Commission proposed a new regulation on circularity requirements for vehicle design and improved management of end-of-life vehicles, in line with the European Green Deal. According to the new regulation, new cars’ components should be easily replaced, reused, recycled, remanufactured, or refurbished. Besides, 20% of new vehicles’ plastic parts should consist in recycled material, in order to reduce waste. There are 285.6 million motor vehicles on EU roads and every year around 6.5 million vehicles come to the end of their lives.

items.title: European Automotive Industry's competitiveness (extended version)
items.summary: This infoclip illustrates the automotive industry from different angles, such as car shipping; manufacturing, car dealers, electric vehicles charging, and a car repair garage. The clip shows the automotive port of Antwerp-Bruges, the Audi factory in Brussels and a car dealer in Brussels city center. In spring 2024, the German manufacturer announced the restructuring of its Brussels plant, provoking protest among employees and trade unions. The European automotive sector is experiencing a crisis sparked by the transition to low carbon technologies, the degradation of supply chains, increased competition from Asia, and decreased demand for electric models.

items.title: Wastewater treatment
items.summary: This infoclip illustrates the water way, from waste water coming from dishes, getting into sewers and water treatment by the Brussels North Aquiris water treatment plant. And its return to the cycle of rivers and streams.

events.title: EC archives of the week

items.title: EC archives of the week (07-13/06): World Food Safety Day (07/06) First European elections (07-10/06/1979, 47 years ago) World Ocean Day (08/06) Portugal National Day (10/06) Visit of Nelson Mandela, President of the African National Congress (ANC), to the CEC (10/06/1991, 35 years ago) Signature of an EU/Slovenia Association Agreement (10/06/1996, 30 years ago) Spain and Portugal join the EU (12/06/1985, 41 years ago) Signature of the Stabilisation and Association Agreement between the EU and Albania (12/06/2006, 20 years ago) Cast-off of the "Treaty of Rome" yacht (13/06/1981, 45 years ago)
